evidence: Check the Agent's final response for the $89,400 Audit & Assurance portion (account 401000) of the $147,825 recognition.

evidence: Check the Agent's final response for the $32,250 Tax Preparation & Planning portion (account 402000) of the $147,825 recognition.

justification: The prompt asks the Agent to validate the figure 'across our service lines.' The Advisory & Consulting credit leg of JE-0057 is $26,175 to account 403000 (verified in ogl_journal_entries.json). (Outcome 2.1 - key fact.)
evidence: Check the Agent's final response for the $26,175 Advisory & Consulting portion (account 403000) of the $147,825 recognition.

title: The Agent reports that the recognized $147,825 ties back to Andrea Phil's May engagement-stage completion review (andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com), which stated three service lines crossed milestones and $147,825 should be recognized for May.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to confirm the recognized figure 'genuinely ties back to the engagement-stage completion review it's supposed to come from.' Verified in emails.json (email_scen_059_wip_recognition_0000) from andrea.phil. (Outcome 2.1 - key fact.)
evidence: Check the Agent's final response for confirmation that the $147,825 recognition ties back to Andrea Phil's (andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com) May engagement-stage completion review, which stated three service lines crossed milestones and $147,825 should be recognized for May.

title: The Agent reports that the recognition ties to the underlying work-in-process / billable-hours support carried in account 119000 (Work in Process - Unbilled Services).
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to confirm the recognition ties 'to the billable hours underneath it' and to walk it back to the underlying detail. Account 119000 'Work in Process - Unbilled Services' holds the unbilled WIP/billable-hours support for the recognition (verified in ogl_accounts.json / blackline_reconciliations.json). This criterion checks that the recognition is grounded in the hours support; it is distinct from #8, which checks that the $89,425 billed AR leg equals that same WIP work. (Outcome 2.1 - key fact.)
evidence: Minimum acceptance bar (any ONE of the following satisfies the criterion): the Agent (a) names account 119000 / 'Work in Process - Unbilled Services' as the source of the billable-hours support behind the recognition, OR (b) cites the clean May time-and-WIP hours feed as the support the recognition is grounded in, OR (c) states the recognized/billed work is the same work being carried as unbilled WIP (or similar). Deconfliction: a statement that ONLY ties the $89,425 AR leg to WIP belongs to #8 and does not by itself satisfy #6 - #6 requires the recognition to be grounded in the 119000 hours support, not just the AR-leg tie-out.

title: The Agent reports that the recognition includes an $89,425 billed receivable booked to Accounts Receivable (account 110000).
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to confirm the recognition ties 'to the receivable that's landed on our ledger.' JE-0057 line 1 debits $89,425 to account 110000 Accounts Receivable - Client Billings (verified in ogl_journal_entries.json). (Outcome 2.1 - key fact.)
evidence: Check the Agent's final response for the $89,425 billed receivable booked to Accounts Receivable (account 110000) as part of the recognition.

title: The Agent reports that the $89,425 billed portion reflects work actually carried in WIP (the billed receivable is the same work being carried as unbilled work-in-process).
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to confirm 'the billed portion is the same work we're actually carrying.' This is a tie-out conclusion specifically between the $89,425 AR leg (account 110000) and the WIP work carried in account 119000, distinct from merely reporting the AR leg exists and distinct from #6 (which checks the recognition is grounded in the 119000 hours support). (Outcome 2.1 - key fact.)
evidence: Check the Agent's final response for confirmation that the $89,425 billed portion (the AR leg booked to account 110000) reflects work actually carried in WIP - i.e., the billed receivable is the same work being carried as unbilled work-in-process. Deconfliction: a general statement that the recognition is grounded in the 119000 hours support belongs to #6; #8 specifically requires equating the $89,425 billed AR leg with that WIP work.

title: The Agent reports that, beyond the $147,825 already posted (JE-0057), the approved-but-unposted WIP recognition entry JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0045 (je_46e6033b6aa946e7), $15,223.56 recognizing to account 401000 (Audit & Assurance), is genuinely-earned time that should be posted before the May lock.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to 'tell me what, if anything, is still genuinely sitting as unbilled time that ought to be recognized before we lock, versus what's just noise.' Beyond the posted $147,825 (JE-0057), the brookfield FP-2026-05 ledger holds an approved-but-unposted WIP-to-revenue entry je_46e6033b6aa946e7 ($15,223.56 -> 401000), described 'WIP recognition - billable time logged', status 'approved', posted_at null (verified in ogl_journal_entries.json). This is genuine earned work that needs posting before the lock, not noise. (Outcome 2.1 - key fact.)
evidence: Check the Agent's final response for identification of JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0045 (je_46e6033b6aa946e7), $15,223.56 to 401000, status 'approved' / posted_at null, as genuinely-earned WIP that should be posted before the May lock. Note for the judge: the submitted-only entry JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0038 (je_1f83fec3cf0346db, $43,950.76, status 'submitted', not yet approved) is NOT postable and must not be substituted for or counted alongside the approved entries; the approved-but-unposted set totals $33,844.93 (this entry plus JE-0031).

title: The Agent reports that, beyond the $147,825 already posted (JE-0057), the approved-but-unposted WIP recognition entry JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0031 (je_01de85923ce744ba), $18,621.37 recognizing to account 403000 (Advisory & Consulting), is genuinely-earned time that should be posted before the May lock.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to 'tell me what, if anything, is still genuinely sitting as unbilled time that ought to be recognized before we lock, versus what's just noise.' Beyond the posted $147,825 (JE-0057), the brookfield FP-2026-05 ledger holds an approved-but-unposted WIP-to-revenue entry je_01de85923ce744ba ($18,621.37 -> 403000), described 'WIP recognition - billable time logged', status 'approved', posted_at null (verified in ogl_journal_entries.json). This is the entry agents most often missed (substituting the submitted-only JE-0038). It is genuine earned work that needs posting before the lock, not noise. (Outcome 2.1 - key fact.)
evidence: Check the Agent's final response for identification of JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0031 (je_01de85923ce744ba), $18,621.37 to 403000, status 'approved' / posted_at null, as genuinely-earned WIP that should be posted before the May lock. Note for the judge: the submitted-only entry JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0038 (je_1f83fec3cf0346db, $43,950.76, status 'submitted', not yet approved) is NOT postable and must not be substituted for this entry; the approved-but-unposted set totals $33,844.93 (this entry plus JE-0045).

title: The Agent explicitly rejects the subledger feed-timing explanation for the approximately $4,390.62 variance, stating it is NOT a feed-timing issue.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent not to take the feed-timing explanation 'at face value.' The surface explanation on recon BL-75810CD0FEE4 attributes the $4,390.62 to a partial-success subledger feed retry left for next-period cleanup; the linked exception exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d shows the real cause is a missing accrual, so the feed-timing story must be explicitly rejected (verified in blackline_reconciliations.json / blackline_exceptions.json). (Outcome 2.1 - key fact.)
evidence: Check the Agent's final response for an explicit statement that the approximately $4,390.62 variance is NOT a subledger feed-timing issue (rejecting the surface 'partial feed / leave for next period' explanation).

title: The Agent identifies that the variance is tracked by BlackLine exception exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d (high urgency, SOX implications), assigned to anaya.wallace@brookfieldcpas.com, as the open item it is waiting on.

title: The Agent logs the variance root cause in a persistent tracking system that will be picked up at the next close, via any one of: a Linear issue, a BlackLine review note or close task on the May close, or an Airtable record in the close/triage workflow.

Per the Oracle Events note, this criterion is kept path-agnostic so a valid Linear / BlackLine / Airtable trajectory is not unfairly failed. (Outcome 1.1 - write-action result.)

Confirm the write action returned success. Any one destination satisfies this criterion.
